title: Foreground Memory-Chain Strand
description: A long hanging strand assembled from alternating heavy memory links, narrow connector rings, repair clamps, and occasional suspended tokens. It is intended for extreme foreground use, where large silhouette changes and shallow depth of field frame the world without feeling like an ordinary industrial chain.
narrativeFunction: The chain field represents the weight that keeps memories from drifting away. Strands form foreground curtains, create camera wipes, add near-field motion, and contrast the lightness of filaments with tangible mass. Individual links may carry scars, names, or attached recollections.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin is the top suspension point. Strand growth proceeds downward along local negative Y, while sway, link twist, and token motion are evaluated around this fixed point.

silhouette.primary: A vertical hanging strand of large alternating oval links, gradually narrowing toward the lower end and bending into a soft weighted curve.
silhouette.secondary: Every fourth or fifth link is interrupted by a thicker repair clamp, connector ring, or token plate that changes the visual rhythm.
silhouette.tertiary: Hammered edge dents, engraved names, wrapped wire, hairline gold-filled cracks, small attached rings, and localized oxidation create close foreground detail.
silhouette.negativeSpace: The hollow center of every major link remains open and legible. Adjacent links rotate approximately ninety degrees and never visually seal each other's openings.
silhouette.recognitionTest: From silhouette the asset reads as a ceremonial weighted chain with irregular memory interventions, never as rope, prayer beads, vines, or a uniform industrial chain.

materials.surfaceDescription: Nearly black forged iron with broad hammered facets, worn contact patches, soot, and subtle violet oxidation inside protected cavities.
Warm gold-bronze used sparingly as structural bands and filled cracks, brighter at worn edges but never mirror polished.
Matte mauve-gray ceramic with recessed inscriptions, chipped corners, and dim internal light leaking only through selected cuts.

components.role: Large alternating structural links carrying the strand silhouette and apparent mass.
components.detail: Scale links into elongated ovals, flatten selected loaded contact zones, alternate orientation by ninety degrees, and vary hammered facets without changing link thickness.

components.role: Smaller rings inserted between major links to break mechanical regularity and expose load transfer.
components.detail: Connector rings are slightly distorted and sit at loaded angles between selected major links instead of floating at mathematically perfect centers.

components.role: Thick metallic bands spanning cracked or overloaded links.
components.detail: Each clamp follows local link curvature, uses two visible fasteners, bridges an actual modeled crack, and remains narrower than the link opening.

components.role: Small inscribed plates attached at irregular points along the strand.
components.detail: Tokens vary among shield, teardrop, broken oval, and clipped photograph profiles and hang from one visibly smaller ring.

components.role: Faceted terminating weight keeping the strand under visible tension.
components.detail: The weight contains a recessed name field, one gold repair line, and a small open lower socket for continuation or ritual ornament.

assemblyOrder: Apply repair clamps to selected stressed links
Attach memory tokens and wrapped-wire details
Add lower weight and author strand curvature
deformationRules: Elongate major links by forty percent along local vertical and compress depth by fifteen percent.
Apply a cumulative lateral bend of up to sixty centimeters over the full strand length.
Scale lower links gradually to eighty-two percent while maintaining material thickness.
variationRules: Repair-clamp positions vary but never occur on adjacent major links.
Token count ranges from two to five and at least one token hangs outside the main strand silhouette.
Whole-strand phase, bend direction, link twist, and token order vary among field instances.

topology.target: Instancing-friendly hard-surface chain topology with reusable link meshes, separate repair hardware, detachable tokens, and predictable pivots for delayed secondary motion.
topology.bevel: Use broad hammered link bevels with flattened contact patches, narrow clamp bevels, and softened ceramic-token edges carrying visible chips.
topology.subdivision: No global subdivision on links. Use local smoothing on tokens and lower weight while retaining faceted forged surfaces.
topology.normals: Weighted normals stabilize heavy link faces and make dents, repairs, and contact flats readable in grazing foreground light.
topology.uv: Use one seamless iron link texture, a gold repair trim strip, unique token inscription islands, and consistent orientation across instanced links.

animation.motionCharacter: Slow heavy motion propagates downward with delay. Links respond through small independent twists while lighter tokens swing farther, settle later, and occasionally catch a narrow warm highlight.
constraints: Adjacent links never separate or interpenetrate visibly.
Bottom sway remains below forty centimeters.

collision.description: Use one loose vertical capsule following the overall strand for camera avoidance only. Individual link collision is unnecessary and too expensive.

sockets.purpose: Primary ceiling, shell, or invisible world attachment.
Optional weight, anchor stone, or continuation strand attachment.
Attachment point for one close-up narrative memory token.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show the full weighted curve, alternating open link shapes, at least two clamps, and one token outside the main vertical silhouette.
orthographicGuidance.side: Demonstrate ninety-degree link alternation and preserve visible openings through both link orientations.
orthographicGuidance.top: Show the compact suspension footprint and the complete lateral and depth sway envelope required around the strand.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Use a close shallow-depth-of-field view exposing hammered facets, gold repairs, open link centers, and overlapping token silhouettes.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: A human hand should pass through a major link; include one hand or forearm reference beside a hero-detail link.

validation: The default strand contains eighteen major links with alternating orientation.
Every major link retains a clearly open center from at least one standard review angle.
Repair clamps follow link curvature and do not float above the surface.
At least one memory token breaks outside the strand's primary silhouette.
Idle motion propagates downward with visible delay while preserving connected spacing.
LOD2 retains alternating link rhythm, one repair-color break, and the weighted curve.
Top and lower sockets export at opposite ends along local Y.
